summary: Contracts wishing to support lookup of data from external sources may, instead of returning the data directly, revert using `OffchainLookup(address sender, string[] urls, bytes callData, bytes4 callbackFunction, bytes extraData)`. Clients supporting this specification then make an RPC call to a URL from `urls`, supplying `callData`, and getting back an opaque byte string `response`. Finally, clients call the function specified by `callbackFunction` on the contract, providing `response` and `extraData`. The contract can then decode and verify the returned data using an implementation-specific method. This mechanism allows for offchain lookups of data in a way that is transparent to clients, and allows contract authors to implement whatever validation is necessary; in many cases this can be
title: CCIP Read—Secure offchain data retrieval
